risks.title: Access Control & Authorization
risks.summary: Restricting access to systems and data to authorized identities, and enforcing least privilege.

risks.title: Identity & Authentication
risks.summary: Verifying the identity of users and services before granting access.

risks.title: Audit Logging & Monitoring
risks.summary: Recording and reviewing security-relevant events to detect and investigate activity.

risks.title: Encryption & Data Protection
risks.summary: Protecting data in transit and at rest with cryptographic controls.

risks.title: Risk Assessment & Management
risks.summary: Identifying, analyzing, and treating risks to systems, data, and objectives.

risks.title: Incident Response
risks.summary: Detecting, responding to, and recovering from security incidents.

risks.title: Change & Configuration Management
risks.summary: Controlling changes to systems, software, and configurations.

risks.title: Asset & Data Classification
risks.summary: Inventorying assets and classifying data by sensitivity.

risks.title: Vulnerability Management
risks.summary: Identifying and remediating technical vulnerabilities and outdated components.

risks.title: Input Validation & Injection Defense
risks.summary: Validating inputs to prevent injection and malformed-data attacks.

risks.title: Governance, Policy & Accountability
risks.summary: Establishing policy, roles, and accountability for security and AI programs.

risks.title: Third-Party & Supply Chain Risk
risks.summary: Managing risks from vendors, partners, and supply-chain components.

risks.title: AI: Prompt Injection
risks.summary: Crafted input overrides the model's instructions, either directly or through poisoned content it reads from RAG, files, or the web.

controls_resolved.title: Logical access security over protected information assets
Protect against threats from outside the system boundary

risks.title: AI: Vector & Embedding Weaknesses
risks.summary: RAG retrieval and embedding stores get manipulated or leak data, through poisoned documents, embedding inversion, or cross-tenant retrieval.

controls_resolved.title: Logical access security over protected information assets
Detect and monitor for new vulnerabilities and configuration changes

risks.title: AI: Sensitive Information Disclosure
risks.summary: The model reveals secrets, PII, or proprietary data in its responses, whether from training data, system context, or other users' data.

controls_resolved.title: Logical access security over protected information assets
Restrict the transmission and movement of information

risks.title: AI: Improper Output Handling
risks.summary: Model output is consumed by downstream systems without validation, which can lead to XSS, SSRF, or remote code execution.

controls_resolved.title: Logical access security over protected information assets
Detect and monitor for new vulnerabilities and configuration changes

risks.title: AI: Misinformation
risks.summary: The model produces confidently wrong or fabricated output that users over-trust, leading to harmful decisions.

controls_resolved.title: Detect and monitor for new vulnerabilities and configuration changes
Identify and develop risk mitigation activities

risks.title: AI: Data & Model Poisoning
risks.summary: Training, fine-tuning, or embedding data is tampered with to implant backdoors, bias, or degraded behavior.

controls_resolved.title: Detect and monitor for new vulnerabilities and configuration changes
Identify and develop risk mitigation activities

risks.title: AI: System Prompt Leakage
risks.summary: The system prompt, which holds rules, secrets, or architecture details, gets extracted through crafted questions.

controls_resolved.title: Logical access security over protected information assets
Restrict the transmission and movement of information

risks.title: AI: Model Theft & Extraction
risks.summary: Unauthorized access to or replication of a proprietary model through query-based extraction or direct exfiltration.

Restrict the transmission and movement of information

risks.title: AI: Unbounded Consumption
risks.summary: Uncontrolled token, compute, or API consumption, including token floods, recursive expansion, runaway cost, and denial of wallet.

risks.title: AI: Supply Chain
risks.summary: Compromised models, datasets, libraries, or plugins introduce vulnerabilities into the AI pipeline.

controls_resolved.title: Assess and manage risks from vendors and business partners

Business Associate Contracts and Other Arrangements

risks.title: AI: API Authentication & Access
risks.summary: The endpoint serving the model lacks auth, leaks keys, or allows unauthenticated access, which is the classic API attack surface.

controls_resolved.title: Logical access security over protected information assets
Manage access based on roles and least privilege

risks.title: AI: Security Misconfiguration
risks.summary: Missing security headers, wildcard CORS, verbose errors, exposed files, or no HTTPS enforcement on the AI service.

controls_resolved.title: Logical access security over protected information assets
Protect against threats from outside the system boundary

risks.title: AI: Excessive Agency
risks.summary: An agent has too much autonomy, permission, or tool access, letting manipulation translate into real-world actions.

controls_resolved.title: Logical access security over protected information assets
Manage access based on roles and least privilege

risks.title: AI: Agentic Tool & Chain Abuse
risks.summary: Multi-step agents get hijacked through tool outputs, memory poisoning, or chained calls that compound a single injection.

Manage access based on roles and least privilege

risks.title: AI: Insufficient Human Oversight
risks.summary: No human in the loop or audit trail on high-impact agent actions, so failures and manipulations go unchecked.

controls_resolved.title: Exfiltration via AI Agent Tool Invocation
Logical access security over protected information assets

risks.title: AI: MCP & Connector Tool Abuse
risks.summary: Untrusted or over-permissioned tool servers (for example MCP servers and third-party connectors) feed poisoned tool output, hijack tool calls, or hand an agent capabilities it should not have. Documented in real autonomous-attack tradecraft where penetration tools were wired in as MCP servers to turn an agent into an attack platform.
